articles.title: Congress keeps kicking surveillance reform down the road
articles.description:
Congress has reauthorized Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act - but only for another 45 days. The extension is meant to give legislators more time to negotiate reforms to the controversial wiretapping bill. If the past few weeks are any indication of how future debates will go, however, we're in for a bumpy ride.
The House renewed Section 702 with minor reforms on Wednesday evening. The bill didn't include the hotly debated warrant requirement, but it did feature a provision prohibiting the Federal Reserve from issuing Central Bank Digital Currencies, which Senate Majority Leader John Thune (R-SD) described as a nonstar …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Trump ends DHS' months-long nightmare that left immigration enforcement without funding
articles.description: President Trump signed a bill ending a record-setting 75-day DHS funding lapse, though ICE and Customs and Border Protection remain unfunded.

articles.title: Apple’s iPhone revenue jumps to $57 billion despite chip shortages
articles.description:
Apple's iPhone revenue jumped 22 percent to $57 billion over the past few months, despite supply chain issues impacting the device's processor. During an interview with Reuters, Apple CEO Tim Cook said iPhone "demand was off the charts" but added that there's "a little less flexibility in the supply chain at the moment for getting more parts."
Apple earned $111.2 billion in revenue during the second quarter of 2026, its "best March quarter ever," according to Cook. This latest earnings report comes a little over a week after the company announced CEO Cook's retirement in September. John Ternus, the company's head of hardware engineering, wi …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Ashwini Vaishnaw flags off Jammu-Srinagar Vande Bharat Express
articles.description: A new 20-coach Vande Bharat Express now connects Jammu and Srinagar directly, significantly cutting travel time to five hours. This direct rail link, a first for the two cities, will operate six days a week starting May 2.

articles.title: GOAT in Kashmir: Swiss Saanen sparks milk rush, but wait is long
articles.description: Srinagar's agricultural university has welcomed Swiss Saanen goats, sparking immense interest for their high milk yield with less feed. These docile animals, capable of producing five to six litres daily, are being studied for their adaptability to Kashmir's climate and potential for crossbreeding with local breeds. Researchers believe they could revolutionize dairy farming, even for urban households.

articles.title: Man in Jordan revives injured butterfly with handmade petal wing after days of care – Watch adorable video
articles.description: A man in Jordan has used dried orchid petals to create a tiny prosthetic wing for an injured butterfly, helping the insect fly again. The delicate repair has since gone viral, drawing attention to how small acts of care can make a real difference for fragile wildlife.

articles.title: Rivian’s revenue is up as R2 production kicks into gear
articles.description:
Rivian reported its first quarter earnings of 2026, providing us a closer look at the company's financial health as it kicks off production for the crucial R2 electric vehicle. 
We've already got Rivian's production and delivery statement from the first three months of the year. The company sold 10,365 vehicles in Q1, representing 20 percent increase year over year. And it produced 10,236 vehicles at its factory in Normal, Illinois, which represents a 30 percent increase compared to the same period last year. The company also reaffirmed its prediction that it will sell 62,000-67,000 vehicles this year.
In terms of earnings, Rivian said it  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Rivian downsizes its goals for its EV factory in Georgia
articles.description:
Rivian announced some changes today with regard to the factory its building in the state of Georgia. The company was planning to build the facility in two phases, each resulting in 200,000 vehicles of annual production capacity, for a total of 400,000 units. 
Now the company says it is only planning for 300,000 units of annual capacity as a result of a revised loan agreement with the US Department of Energy - though its planning on hitting that annual capacity sooner than originally planned. DOE will now loan Rivian $4.5 billion, instead of the originally agreed amount of $6.6 billion, announced in the last days of the Biden administration. …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: The logic of the racist Supreme Court isn’t adding up
articles.description:
Close watchers of the Supreme Court knew that the conservative supermajority was about to murder what was left of the Voting Rights Act. Wednesday's decision in Louisiana v. Callais took down Section 2 of the law, clearing the way for racist gerrymandering, because it is now racist to remedy racism. The decision is an affront to the history of the Voting Rights Act, an affront to the history of the United States, and an affront to math. 
The state of Louisiana, which is around 30 percent Black, has six districts. The voting districts are drawn so that there are two majority-Black districts. That is two out of six districts; approximately 33 …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Microsoft’s Xbox mode is now available for all Windows 11 PCs
articles.description:
Microsoft is now rolling out its Xbox mode to all Windows 11 PCs. The new Xbox mode adds a full-screen interface to the Xbox PC app, much like Steam's Big Picture Mode, and originally debuted as the Xbox Full Screen Experience (FSE) with Asus' Xbox Ally devices.
"Some players in select markets will be able to download the Xbox mode experience today, with availability expanding to more players in those markets over the next several weeks," says the Xbox team. You'll be able to get the Xbox mode by installing the latest Windows update for Windows 11.
Xbox mode aims to try and bridge the gap between Xbox consoles and Windows, but its original …

articles.title: ‘A new chapter’: first commercial fight from US since 2019 lands in Venezuela
articles.description:
Four months after US capture of Nicolás Maduro, officials hail repairing of ties as American Airlines flight lands at Simón Bolívar airport
US and Venezuelan officials have hailed a new era in diplomatic relations as the first direct commercial flight between the two countries in more than seven years landed in Caracas.
Nearly four months ago, US special forces attack helicopters and planes swept into the skies over Venezuela’s capital after Donald Trump ordered the capture of its president, Nicolás Maduro.
 Continue reading...

articles.title: Congress keeps kicking surveillance reform down the road
articles.description:
Congress has reauthorized Section 702 of the Foreign Intelligence Surveillance Act - but only for another 45 days. The extension is meant to give legislators more time to negotiate reforms to the controversial wiretapping bill. If the past few weeks are any indication of how future debates will go, however, we're in for a bumpy ride.
The House renewed Section 702 with minor reforms on Wednesday evening. The bill didn't include the hotly debated warrant requirement, but it did feature a provision prohibiting the Federal Reserve from issuing Central Bank Digital Currencies, which Senate Majority Leader John Thune (R-SD) described as a nonstar …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Trump ends DHS' months-long nightmare that left immigration enforcement without funding
articles.description: President Trump signed a bill ending a record-setting 75-day DHS funding lapse, though ICE and Customs and Border Protection remain unfunded.

articles.title: Apple’s iPhone revenue jumps to $57 billion despite chip shortages
articles.description:
Apple's iPhone revenue jumped 22 percent to $57 billion over the past few months, despite supply chain issues impacting the device's processor. During an interview with Reuters, Apple CEO Tim Cook said iPhone "demand was off the charts" but added that there's "a little less flexibility in the supply chain at the moment for getting more parts."
Apple earned $111.2 billion in revenue during the second quarter of 2026, its "best March quarter ever," according to Cook. This latest earnings report comes a little over a week after the company announced CEO Cook's retirement in September. John Ternus, the company's head of hardware engineering, wi …
Read the full story at The Verge.

articles.title: Ashwini Vaishnaw flags off Jammu-Srinagar Vande Bharat Express
articles.description: A new 20-coach Vande Bharat Express now connects Jammu and Srinagar directly, significantly cutting travel time to five hours. This direct rail link, a first for the two cities, will operate six days a week starting May 2.

articles.title: GOAT in Kashmir: Swiss Saanen sparks milk rush, but wait is long
articles.description: Srinagar's agricultural university has welcomed Swiss Saanen goats, sparking immense interest for their high milk yield with less feed. These docile animals, capable of producing five to six litres daily, are being studied for their adaptability to Kashmir's climate and potential for crossbreeding with local breeds. Researchers believe they could revolutionize dairy farming, even for urban households.

articles.title: Air Canada Suspends 2026 Guidance Amid Rising Fuel Prices
articles.description: Air Canada suspended its full-year 2026 guidance due to uncertainty related to jet fuel prices.

articles.title: Modder releases loader to turn the PS5 into a Linux system
articles.description: Linux is having a very busy year.

articles.title: Man in Jordan revives injured butterfly with handmade petal wing after days of care – Watch adorable video
articles.description: A man in Jordan has used dried orchid petals to create a tiny prosthetic wing for an injured butterfly, helping the insect fly again. The delicate repair has since gone viral, drawing attention to how small acts of care can make a real difference for fragile wildlife.

articles.title: Trumps says Iran can play at World Cup in US after FIFA confirmation
articles.description: FIFA chief Gianni Infantino has said Iran will play their World Cup matches, leading to agreement from US President.

articles.title: Iran will be at the World Cup and will play in the US, FIFA boss says
articles.description: "We have to bring people together," FIFA President Gianni Infantino said.

articles.title: Iran to play World Cup games in USA as planned insists FIFA president
articles.description: Iran will play their World Cup matches in the United States, FIFA president Gianni Infantino has insisted.

topStories.title: Chick-fil-A employee busted in bizarre $80K mac-and-cheese theft scheme
topStories.description: Keyshun Jones faces theft and money laundering charges after allegedly stealing $80,000 from a Texas Chick-fil-A through a fake refund scheme.

topStories.title: Britain warns another terror attack is 'highly likely' within 6 months after London stabbing
topStories.description: Britain raised its national terror threat level to severe after two people were stabbed in an antisemitic attack in London's Golders Green neighborhood.

topStories.title: Oil prices hit 4-year high amid Iran war
topStories.description: Oil surged overnight Wednesday into Thursday to $126 a barrel, before eventually falling. CBS News MoneyWatch correspondent Kelly O'Grady reports.

topStories.title: STAT+: In her own words: Surgeon general nominee Nicole Saphier expresses enthusiasm and caution for MAHA
topStories.description: Examining the past statements and writings of Trump's third surgeon general nominee, Dr. Nicole Saphier, on vaccines, autism, peptides, diet and more.

topStories.title: FCC boss Brendan Carr maintains early ABC license renewal is about DEI probe, not Jimmy Kimmel controversy
topStories.description: FCC Chairman Carr says early renewal of Disney's ABC licenses is tied to a DEI review, not President Trump's call for Jimmy Kimmel's firing over his controversial joke.

topStories.title: US-Venezuela direct flights resume after seven-year suspension
topStories.description: The first direct US-Venezuela commercial flight in seven years departed Miami for Caracas on Thursday.

topStories.description: Jeremiyah Love said he won't spend any NFL game checks, revealing his plan to use marketing money to take care of his family before himself.

dailyNews.description: Intel's market cap more than doubled in April, boosted by a blowout earnings report and optimism that the chipmaker is in the midst of a revival.

dailyNews.description: President Trump took questions from reporters at the Oval Office on Thursday after signing an executive order on retirement planning. CBS News senior White House and political correspondent Ed O'Keefe joins following the president's remarks.

dailyNews.title: Senators ban themselves from participating in prediction markets
dailyNews.description: The platforms, which have gained scrutiny recently, allow people to place bets on future events such as election results. The ban also applies to Senate staffers.

dailyNews.title: Rivian’s revenue is up as R2 production kicks into gear
dailyNews.description:
Rivian reported its first quarter earnings of 2026, providing us a closer look at the company's financial health as it kicks off production for the crucial R2 electric vehicle. 
We've already got Rivian's production and delivery statement from the first three months of the year. The company sold 10,365 vehicles in Q1, representing 20 percent increase year over year. And it produced 10,236 vehicles at its factory in Normal, Illinois, which represents a 30 percent increase compared to the same period last year. The company also reaffirmed its prediction that it will sell 62,000-67,000 vehicles this year.
In terms of earnings, Rivian said it  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

dailyNews.title: Rivian downsizes its goals for its EV factory in Georgia
dailyNews.description:
Rivian announced some changes today with regard to the factory its building in the state of Georgia. The company was planning to build the facility in two phases, each resulting in 200,000 vehicles of annual production capacity, for a total of 400,000 units. 
Now the company says it is only planning for 300,000 units of annual capacity as a result of a revised loan agreement with the US Department of Energy - though its planning on hitting that annual capacity sooner than originally planned. DOE will now loan Rivian $4.5 billion, instead of the originally agreed amount of $6.6 billion, announced in the last days of the Biden administration. …
Read the full story at The Verge.

dailyNews.title: Supreme Court’s Voting Rights Ruling Could Fuel New Gerrymandering Era
dailyNews.description: The expected flood of new congressional maps is likely to produce fewer competitive districts, fewer ways for voters to hold elected officials accountable and more polarized politics.

dailyNews.title: The logic of the racist Supreme Court isn’t adding up
dailyNews.description:
Close watchers of the Supreme Court knew that the conservative supermajority was about to murder what was left of the Voting Rights Act. Wednesday's decision in Louisiana v. Callais took down Section 2 of the law, clearing the way for racist gerrymandering, because it is now racist to remedy racism. The decision is an affront to the history of the Voting Rights Act, an affront to the history of the United States, and an affront to math. 
The state of Louisiana, which is around 30 percent Black, has six districts. The voting districts are drawn so that there are two majority-Black districts. That is two out of six districts; approximately 33 …
Read the full story at The Verge.

dailyNews.title: Texas Summer Camp Hit by Deadly Floods Cancels Reopening Plans
dailyNews.description: Camp Mystic canceled plans to reopen this summer after days of dramatic hearings over its response to deadly Texas floods last year that killed 25 campers and two counselors.

dailyNews.title: Trump pulls Casey Means' stalled surgeon general nomination, announces new pick
dailyNews.description: President Trump says he's nominating former Fox News Channel contributor Dr. Nicole Saphier for surgeon general after Dr. Casey Means' path forward stalled in the Senate over questions about her experience and her stance on vaccines.

dailyNews.title: Tracking who Trump is appointing to fill key administration roles
dailyNews.description: Follow President Trump’s progress filling over 800 positions, among about 1,300 that require Senate confirmation, in this tracker from The Washington Post and the Partnership for Public Service.

topStories.title: Hegseth argues Iran ceasefire ‘pauses’ deadline for Congress’s approval
topStories.description: The War Powers Act requires that the Trump administration seek lawmakers’ consent to continue hostilities beyond 60 days, a time limit that expires Friday.

topStories.title: ‘A new chapter’: first commercial fight from US since 2019 lands in Venezuela
topStories.description:
Four months after US capture of Nicolás Maduro, officials hail repairing of ties as American Airlines flight lands at Simón Bolívar airport
US and Venezuelan officials have hailed a new era in diplomatic relations as the first direct commercial flight between the two countries in more than seven years landed in Caracas.
Nearly four months ago, US special forces attack helicopters and planes swept into the skies over Venezuela’s capital after Donald Trump ordered the capture of its president, Nicolás Maduro.
 Continue reading...

topStories.title: Mapping where the redistricting fight stands and where it’s headed
topStories.description: As Trump pushes for a more Republican-friendly House map, more than half a dozen states are potential targets for mid-decade tweaks to congressional boundaries.

topStories.title: King Charles set to return home with a quiet sense of mission accomplished
topStories.description: LONDON — As King Charles III closed out his four-day state visit to the U.S. on Thursday, President Donald Trump issued a three-word assessment of the monarch.“The greatest king,” Trump said after he and first lady Melania Trump said goodbye to Charles and Queen Camilla, an appraisal that explains why Charles could well be returning to the U.K. feeling a quiet sense of mission accomplished

dailyNews.title: Iran war pushes oil prices to 4-year high as Hegseth faces lawmakers
dailyNews.description: Oil prices hit a 4-year high as Axios reports Trump will hear new options to try to break the Strait of Hormuz standoff with Iran with a new wave of attacks.

dailyNews.title: Trump drops Casey Means as surgeon general nominee, opts for radiologist Nicole Saphier
dailyNews.description: Trump slammed Sen. Bill Cassidy for blocking his surgeon general pick, as he nominated a replacement.

dailyNews.title: Trump Withdraws Nomination of Casey Means for Surgeon General
dailyNews.description: Dr. Means’s nomination had stalled in part over her views on vaccines. The president said he was instead nominating Dr. Nicole B. Saphier, a radiologist at Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center.

dailyNews.title: Kimmel says Donald and Melania Trump are ‘closer than ever’ after ‘expectant widow’ joke backlash
dailyNews.description: Jimmy Kimmel continued his mockery of President Donald Trump and first lady Melania Trump on air, joking about their relationship after his controversial "expectant widow" comment.

dailyNews.title: Trump weighs in on LIV Golf, PGA Tour after Saudis pull funding of startup league
dailyNews.description: President Donald Trump reacted to LIV Golf losing Saudi PIF funding, saying golfers should reunite on the PGA Tour and fans want to see the best players face each other.

dailyNews.title: Trump calls Sen Bill Cassidy 'very disloyal' and urges Louisiana voters to replace him over nominee fight
dailyNews.description: President Trump calls Sen. Bill Cassidy very disloyal for opposing Casey Means' Surgeon General nomination and urges Louisiana voters to replace him.

dailyNews.title: Trump says U.S. may cut the number of American troops in Germany
dailyNews.description: President Trump said Wednesday he is considering reducing the number of U.S. forces in Germany, amid a spat with Germany's chancellor and the NATO alliance over Iran.

dailyNews.title: Trump signs DHS funding bill, ending shutdown for most of agency, including TSA
dailyNews.description: Trump on Thursday signed legislation to fund the Department of Homeland Security after the House approved the bill earlier in the afternoon.

dailyNews.title: Trump said his blockade would cause Iran's oil industry to 'explode' this week. Why that won't happen
dailyNews.description: The U.S. and Iran are locked in a standoff that will only break when one side decides it can no longer tolerate the pain. Who will move is unclear.

dailyNews.title: Trump signs executive order expanding retirement account access for workers
dailyNews.description: The president's executive order expands retirement account access for workers and will be integrated with the Saver's Match.

dailyNews.title: Trump says 'I don't think about' another assassination attempt
dailyNews.description: Trump also said he was not inclined to wear a bulletproof vest, as some people have suggested after the arrest of a gunman over the weekend.
